summary: sendgrid-sdk postinstall beacons installer host identifiers to hardcoded IP
details: sendgrid-sdk is an empty package (index.js is `module.exports = {}`) whose name impersonates Twilio's official @sendgrid/* npm scope. On `npm install`, the postinstall script in package.json executes `node -e` code that performs an HTTP GET to http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping carrying the installer's hostname, CI environment flag, Node.js version, and package version. The beacon fires unconditionally during install, transmits host identifiers in cleartext to a bare IP address, and the package provides no SendGrid functionality to justify any network activity. The combination of a typosquat name targeting a widely-used official scope, no legitimate package contents, and an install-time exfiltration beacon to an attacker-controlled IP is a supply-chain attack against developers who mistype the SendGrid package name. The README self-describing as a 'honeypot / bug bounty PoC' does not change the installer-side impact: every install leaks machine identifiers to a third-party endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
